urgency.message: 228 critical CVE(s) and 2 new KEV addition(s) in the last 7 days. Review immediately.

items.title: Scattered Spider Teens Convicted of TfL Cyber-Attack
items.summary: Two young British men have pleaded guilty to hacking Transport for London as part of a Scattered Spider plot

items.title: Canadian Electricity Provider London Hydro Discloses Data Breach
items.summary:
Hackers stole customers’ names, addresses, email addresses, phone numbers, and account information.
The post Canadian Electricity Provider London Hydro Discloses Data Breach appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: Malicious npm Packages Pose as PostCSS Tools to Deliver Windows RAT
items.summary:
Cybersecurity researchers have discovered a set of malicious npm packages that are designed to deliver a Windows-based remote access trojan (RAT).

The list of identified packages, is below -


  aes-decode-runner-pro (145 downloads)
  postcss-minify-selector (256 downloads)
  postcss-minify-selecto

items.title: Trump Signs Executive Order Accelerating Post-Quantum Cryptography Migration
items.summary:
Federal agencies are required to transition high-value assets and high-impact systems to use PQC by the end of 2030 and 2031.
The post Trump Signs Executive Order Accelerating Post-Quantum Cryptography Migration  appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: Five Eyes Group Issues Urgent Call to Tackle Frontier AI Threats
items.summary: The Five Eyes Alliance has published a rare call to action for organizations facing AI threats

items.title: Xsolis Data Breach Affects 1.4 Million Individuals
items.summary:
Threat actors gained access to personal and protected health information that Xsolis received from its clients.
The post Xsolis Data Breach Affects 1.4 Million Individuals appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: WhatsApp VBScript Campaign Uses Fake Documents to Install ManageEngine RMM Tool
items.summary:
Direct messages sent via WhatsApp are being used to distribute malicious Visual Basic Script (VBScript) files that lead to the installation of legitimate Remote Monitoring and Management (RMM) software.

Per findings from Kaspersky, the active campaign is targeting users of WhatsApp Desktop and What

items.title: OpenAI Expands Daybreak With GPT-5.5-Cyber to Help Defenders Patch Security Flaws
items.summary:
OpenAI on Monday said it's releasing an improved version of its GPT‑5.5‑Cyber model to trusted defenders as part of the Daybreak initiative the artificial intelligence (AI) company announced last month.

Calling GPT‑5.5‑Cyber its "strongest model yet for finding and helping patch software vulne

WhatsApp phishing attack uses fake business docs to hack PCs
items.summary: An ongoing malware campaign is targeting WhatsApp users in multiple countries with deceptive messages that push VBScript files, leading to remote system access. [...]

items.title: The Global Namespace Risk: Universal Bucket Hijacking Technique for Cloud Data Exfiltration
items.summary:
Unit 42 research details how attackers could exploit global name uniqueness in bucket hijacking to redirect cloud data streams across major CSPs.
The post The Global Namespace Risk: Universal Bucket Hijacking Technique for Cloud Data Exfiltration appeared first on Unit 42.

items.title: JaredFromSubway MEV bot hacked in $15 million crypto theft
items.summary: The JaredFromSubway Ethereum MEV (Maximal Extractable Value) bot suffered a $15 million loss after an attacker manipulated the opportunity-detection logic by creating fake cryptocurrency trading opportunities. [...]

items.title: FFmpeg fixes PixelSmash flaw in widely used video decoder
items.summary: A newly disclosed FFmpeg flaw dubbed 'PixelSmash' could be exploited for remote code execution on Jellyfin servers under certain conditions, and can also trigger a denial-of-service  condition in applications like Kodi, Emby, Nextcloud, PhotoPrism, and OBS Studio. [...]

items.title: FortiBleed campaign used custom FortiGate sniffer to steal credentials
items.summary: Security firm SOCRadar says the large-scale FortiBleed campaign targeting Fortinet FortiGate devices used custom sniffers to harvest authentication secrets from compromised firewalls and steal credentials. [...]

items.title: CNAPP’s New Normal: Hyper-Prioritization and Autonomous Remediation at Cloud Scale
items.summary: AI-powered detection has crossed a threshold. Security teams can now surface vulnerabilities, misconfigurations, and active attack paths at a speed and scale that was unimaginable a few years ago. The problem is no longer finding or knowing risk; it’s closing it fast enough to matter. Cloud deployme

items.title: ShapedPlugin WordPress Pro Plugins Backdoored in Supply Chain Attack
items.summary:
Multiple WordPress plugins from ShapedPlugin were compromised in a supply chain attack after unknown threat actors managed to tamper with the official release channels and push backdoor code.

"Attackers compromised the vendor's build and distribution pipeline, injecting backdoor code into Pro plugi

items.title: Microsoft says Windows 11 26H2 is coming soon, details upgrade process
items.summary: Microsoft has confirmed that Windows 11 version 26H2 will be the next feature update and that devices running Windows 11 24H2 and 25H2 will be able to upgrade using a small enablement package. [...]

items.title: Microsoft fixes AutoGen Studio flaw that enabled code execution
items.summary: A vulnerability chain dubbed AutoJack in Microsoft's AutoGen Studio interface for prototyping AI agents could let attackers manipulate an agent into executing arbitrary commands on its host system simply by visiting a malicious webpage. [...]

items.title: OpenAI Launches Full-Scale Effort to Patch Open-Source Bugs as It Takes on Anthropic’s Mythos
items.summary: Amid concerns about AI models’ cybersecurity capabilities, OpenAI revealed an improved version of GPT-5.5-Cyber and its “Patch the Planet” initiative to fix open-source software bugs.

items.title: Researchers Detail DifyTap Flaws in Dify That Could Expose AI Chats Across Tenants
items.summary: Cybersecurity researchers have disclosed details of four vulnerabilities in Dify, an open-source agentic workflow platform with more than 146,000 GitHub stars, that could allow attackers to stealthily read artificial intelligence (AI) conversions from other customers' applications without requiring

items.title: Crypto Heist Fueled by Elaborate Fake Reputation-Boosting Campaign
items.summary: Attackers are using multiple online channels — including GitHub, YouTube, and VirusTotal — to build an illusion of trust to spread a cross-platform clipboard hijacker.

items.summary: ESET details GentleKiller, the EDR-killer framework the Gentlemen ransomware gang gives affiliates

items.title: 29-Year-Old Squid Proxy Bug 'Squidbleed' Can Leak Cleartext HTTP Requests
items.summary:
A heap over-read in the Squid web proxy can leak another user's cleartext HTTP request, including any credentials or session tokens it carries, to anyone already allowed to send traffic through the same proxy.

The bug traces to a 1997 FTP-parsing change and is still live in Squid's default configur

items.title: Webshells Remain Popular, (Mon, Jun 22nd)
items.summary: Webshells have been popular for a long time. We already covered this topic across multiple diaries[1][2]. I spent some time to track them[3] and slighly paid less attention to them but today I found another one. It seems to be a new player (pushed on Github two months a

items.title: Suspected cyberattack triggers false emergency alerts across parts of Brazil
items.summary: The incident occurred early Saturday when at least a dozen unauthorized alerts were sent through Brazil's Civil Defense Alert system, a platform designed to warn residents about imminent threats such as floods, landslides and other natural disasters.

items.title: A Glimpse into the “Search Your Target” Market for Stolen Credentials
items.summary: Attackers no longer need to sift through massive credential dumps. They can pay others to do it for them. Flare explores how an emerging underground market searches stolen credential databases for specific companies, domains, and accounts. [...]

items.title: Unpatchable BootROM Flaw Impacts Apple A12, A13 Chips
items.summary: Apple BootROM exploit exposes unpatchable USB flaw on A12 and A13 devices

items.title: Decades-Old Squid Proxy Flaw ‘Squidbleed’ Can Expose User Data
items.summary:
Squidbleed, discovered with the aid of Claude Mythos Preview, has been described as a Heartbleed-style vulnerability. 
The post Decades-Old Squid Proxy Flaw ‘Squidbleed’ Can Expose User Data appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: New OXLOADER Loader Uses Malicious Google Ads to Deliver CastleStealer
items.summary:
Cybersecurity researchers have disclosed details of a new campaign that delivers CastleStealer by means of a previously unreported malware loader dubbed OXLOADER.

According to Elastic Security Labs, the campaign leverages malicious Google Ads as a starting point to distribute the malware. Evidence

items.title: Google Sets Sept. 30 Deadline for Android Developer Verification in Four Countries
items.summary:
Google has set September 30, 2026, as the day it begins enforcing Android developer verification in the first four countries, and the major device-maker app stores are in from the start.

On that date, certified Android phones in Brazil, Indonesia, Singapore, and Thailand will block normal

Vulnerable WordPress plugin iterations leak API keys, secrets, tokens, server information, and other data.

items.title: Microsoft Attributes Mastra AI Supply Chain Attack to North Korea
items.summary: North Korean threat actor Sapphire Sleet has been linked to a supply chain attack targeting Mastra, according to Microsoft security researchers

items.title: North Korean Hackers Blamed for Mastra NPM Supply Chain Attack
items.summary:
A malicious dependency the attackers added to over 140 Mastra packages fetches a payload targeting cryptocurrency extensions.
The post North Korean Hackers Blamed for Mastra NPM Supply Chain Attack appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: What the Latest ShinyHunters Breaches Reveal About Modern Cyberattacks
items.summary:
Groups like ShinyHunters are demonstrating that attackers do not necessarily need malware or zero-day exploits to cause massive damage.
The post What the Latest ShinyHunters Breaches Reveal About Modern Cyberattacks appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: Klue Breach Enables Hackers to Compromise Cybersecurity Firms via OAuth Tokens
items.summary: At least four cybersecurity firms confirmed they have been affected by a breach of business intelligence platform Klue via Salesforce integration

items.title: New Exploit Bypasses Apple’s Boot Defenses, Affects Millions of iPhones
items.summary:
The vulnerability exploited by the Usbliter8 exploit cannot be patched and a PoC exploit has been released by researchers.

items.title: World Cup Scams Are Getting Harder to Spot
items.summary: From fake tickets to cloned websites, AI is magnifying World Cup scams. Can fans distinguish between what’s real and what’s not?

items.summary: The NCSC has released guidance for Fortinet customers impacted by the FortiBleed threat campaign
